text: The surgeon incises the peritoneum at the base of the sigmoid mesentery, beginning the procedure with the identification of the inferior mesenteric artery.

The surgeon detects the inferior mesenteric artery (IMA) circumferentially and continues dissection from medial to lateral, separating the mesocolon from the retroperitoneum. The IMA and its terminal branches are lifted, revealing the left colic artery in a configuration resembling the letter T.
The surgeon continues proximal dissection of the left colic artery, revealing the inferior mesenteric vein (IMV). The left colic artery and IMV are carefully separated from surrounding tissues.
The surgeon seals the inferior mesenteric vein (IMV) using an energy device, leading to the exposure of the left colic vein.
The surgeon continues the dissection, mobilizing the left colon laterally, separating it from the surrounding tissues.
The surgeon inserts the end of a stapler to divide the sigmoid colon distal to the sigmoid junction. The visual shows the complete dissection of the area with the left colic artery preserved.
The surgeon completes the procedure by performing a Knight-Griffen anastomosis, visually connecting the bowel ends to restore continuity after resection.
